# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based frontend application built with TypeScript and Vite. It follows a component-driven architecture using shadcn/ui for consistent UI components. The application integrates with Supabase for backend services including database, authentication, and serverless functions.

### Key Architectural Decisions

- **Frontend Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript for type safety and better developer experience
- **Build System**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Routing**: React Router for client-side navigation
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state management, React Context for global state
- **UI Library**: shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives for accessibility and consistency
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- **Backend**: Supabase providing PostgreSQL database, authentication, and edge functions
- **Rich Text**: Tiptap editor for HR policy management

## Core Components and Their Purposes

### Layout Components
- `AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout with sidebar and topbar
- `AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with menu items
- `TopBar.tsx`: Top navigation bar with user actions

### Feature Components
- `BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variant support (default/sidebar, with/without wordmark)
- `NavLink.tsx`: Enhanced NavLink component with active state styling
- `SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown with search functionality
- `EvaluationTimeline.tsx`: Timeline view of employee evaluations (quarterly and daily)
- `RichTextEditor.tsx`: Rich text editor for HR policies using Tiptap

### Settings Components
- `AttendanceTab.tsx`: Attendance configuration settings
- `CompanyTab.tsx`: Company information settings
- `DangerZoneTab.tsx`: Critical settings (deletion, etc.)
- `DepartmentsTab.tsx`: Department management
- `EvaluationParametersTab.tsx`: Evaluation criteria configuration
- `ExpenseCategoriesTab.tsx`: Expense category management
- `RolesTab.tsx`: Role and permission management

### UI Components
Extensive use of shadcn/ui components including:
- Form controls (Button, Input, Select, etc.)
- Layout components (Card, Sheet, Dialog, etc.)
- Data display (Table, Badge, Avatar, etc.)
- Navigation (Tabs, Breadcrumb, etc.)
- Feedback (Toast, Alert, Skeleton, etc.)

## Business Logic and Data Flow

### Authentication and Authorization
- Uses Supabase Auth for user authentication
- Role-based access control with different permission levels:
  - CEO: Full access
  - HR Manager: HR operations access
  - Team Lead: Team management access
  - Employee: Limited self-service access

### Employee Management
- Employee profiles with avatar, designation, department
- Searchable employee selection across the application
- Organizational hierarchy management

### Evaluation System
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Comprehensive performance reviews with scores, comments, and recommendations
- **Daily Evaluations**: Quick feedback system with direction (positive/constructive) and remarks
- Timeline view showing evaluation history
- Role-based visibility controls for evaluations

### HR Policies
- Rich text content management using Tiptap editor
- Support for formatting (bold, italic, headings, lists, links)
- Policy creation and editing interface

### Settings Management
- Company-wide configuration options
- Department and role structure management
- Attendance and evaluation parameter configuration
- Expense category definitions

## Database Schema (Supabase)

### Core Tables
- `employees`: Employee profiles and organizational data
- `evaluations`: Quarterly performance evaluations
- `daily_evaluations`: Daily feedback entries
- `hr_policies`: Company policy documents
- `departments`: Organizational departments
- `roles`: User roles and permissions
- `attendance`: Attendance records
- `expense_categories`: Expense classification

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to departments and have roles
- Evaluations reference employees and evaluators
- Daily evaluations have reviewer and reviewee relationships
- Policies are company-wide documents

## Key Files and Entry Points

### Application Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Main application entry point, renders App component
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Root page component

### Configuration Files
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration

### Integration Files
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client setup
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Database type definitions

### Utility Files
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for className merging)
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control logic
- `src/lib/format-role.ts`: Role formatting utilities

## Development Workflow

- Uses ESLint for code linting
- Vitest for unit testing
- Playwright for end-to-end testing
- Supabase CLI for database management and migrations

## Deployment Considerations

- Built as a static SPA deployable to any static hosting service
- Environment variables for Supabase configuration
- Database migrations managed through Supabase
- Edge functions for server-side logic

## Security Considerations

- Row Level Security (RLS) policies in Supabase
- Role-based access control in application logic
- Secure handling of sensitive employee data
- Authentication required for all operations
